growth_records.medium: Diluted whey (DW) medium, initial pH adjusted to 7.0

up.method: total-sugars assay over time (rates only shown graphically vs pH)

sec.units: g/L (endpoint titre, not a specific rate)

growth_records.cite: Guerra, Nelson Perez et al. Modelling the Biphasic Growth and Product Formation by Enterococcus faecium CECT 410 in Realkalized Fed-Batch Fermentations in Whey. Journal of Biomedicine and Biotechnology (2010). DOI:10.1155/2010/290286. [PMC2896665]
growth_records.snippet: The higher concentrations of biomass (0.24 g/L) and final viable cell concentrations (9 x 10^8 CFU/mL) were produced at an initial pH value of 7.0 after 12 h of incubation ... the maximum amount produced (0.9 g/L at initial pH 7.0)
growth_records.notes: Batch shake-flask screen of initial pH (4.5,5.0,5.5,6.0,6.5,7.0,7.5) on diluted-whey (DW) medium; numeric endpoint values are given only for the best condition (initial pH 7.0): biomass 0.24 g/L and lactic acid 0.9 g/L, viable count 9x10^8 CFU/mL at 12 h. Fermentation is homolactic (lactic acid the only product). No numeric specific uptake/secretion rates in text: instantaneous growth and nutrient-consumption rates were computed per sampling time but reported only graphically versus pH (Figures). Growth-rate field left null because no mu was tabulated for the batch runs. Medium (DW) composition per Methods (g/L): lactose 22.0, total N 0.45, total P 0.25, proteins 2.0, native pH 5.4 then adjusted to 7.0. Inoculum 2% (v/v) of a 12-h DW culture.

growth_records.medium: Realkalized fed-batch fermentation I: DW medium (initial pH 7.0) fed with 400 g/L concentrated lactose

up.method: restored to 22 g/L each feeding cycle; no carbon limitation; per-cycle consumption tracked but specific rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Guerra, Nelson Perez et al. Modelling the Biphasic Growth and Product Formation by Enterococcus faecium CECT 410 in Realkalized Fed-Batch Fermentations in Whey. Journal of Biomedicine and Biotechnology (2010). DOI:10.1155/2010/290286. [PMC2896665]
growth_records.snippet: the final productions of biomass (1.3 g/L), lactic acid (3.4 g/L), acetic acid (0.6 g/L), ethanol (2.7 g/L), butane-2,3-diol (0.6 g/L), and the viable cell concentrations (4.0 x 10^9 CFU/mL) obtained at the end of this first fed-batch cultu
growth_records.notes: Realkalized fed-batch I on DW medium fed with 400 g/L concentrated lactose. Endpoint titres: biomass 1.3 g/L, lactic acid 3.4, acetic acid 0.6, ethanol 2.7, butane-2,3-diol 0.6 g/L, 4.0x10^9 CFU/mL. Growth is biphasic; the two max specific growth rates come from the double-Monod model fit (Table 4): mu_max1 = 0.130 +/- 0.0341 h^-1 (phase 1), mu_max2 = 0.300 +/- 0.0215 h^-1 (phase 2). growth_rate_per_h recorded as the phase-2 mu_max = 0.300 h^-1; doubling_time = ln2/0.300 = 0.6931/0.300 = 2.31 h. growth_method=calculated (model-fit, not a directly measured OD slope). These mu values are MODEL PARAMETERS, not raw measurements. Secretion values are final concentrations (g/L), not specific secretion rates. Text states no carbon-source limitation because total sugars were restored to 22 g/L each cycle. Growth stopped ~168 h, attributed to N/P exhaustion.

growth_records.medium: Realkalized fed-batch fermentation II: DW medium (initial pH 7.0) fed with a mixture of 400 g/L concentrated lactose and concentrated whey (CW) medium

up.method: restored to 22 g/L each feeding cycle; no carbon limitation; specific rate not tabulated

growth_records.cite: Guerra, Nelson Perez et al. Modelling the Biphasic Growth and Product Formation by Enterococcus faecium CECT 410 in Realkalized Fed-Batch Fermentations in Whey. Journal of Biomedicine and Biotechnology (2010). DOI:10.1155/2010/290286. [PMC2896665]
growth_records.snippet: the final productions of biomass (3.6 g/L), lactic acid (31.7 g/L), acetic acid (2.8 g/L), ethanol (4.5 g/L), butane-2,3-diol (4.0 g/L), and the viable cell counts (1.1 x 10^10 CFU/mL) obtained in this second fed-batch culture
growth_records.notes: Realkalized fed-batch II on DW medium fed with 400 g/L lactose + concentrated whey (CW) medium (replenishing N and P). Endpoint titres: biomass 3.6 g/L, lactic acid 31.7, acetic acid 2.8, ethanol 4.5, butane-2,3-diol 4.0 g/L, 1.1x10^10 CFU/mL. Double-Monod model fit (Table 4): mu_max1 = 0.365 +/- 0.0653 h^-1 (phase 1), mu_max2 = 0.118 +/- 0.0108 h^-1 (phase 2). growth_rate_per_h recorded as the phase-1 mu_max = 0.365 h^-1 (the dominant/higher value here); doubling_time = ln2/0.365 = 0.6931/0.365 = 1.90 h. growth_method=calculated (model parameter, not measured OD slope). Secretion values are final concentrations (g/L), not specific secretion rates. Homolactic phase lasted 192 h (vs 108 h in fed-batch I) thanks to CW N/P supplementation; growth stopped ~324 h at N exhaustion (P not fully consumed). CW feed composition per Methods (g/L): lactose 48.1, total N 1.05, total P 0.43, proteins 5.0.
